seoTitle: Write Off Bad Debt in QuickBooks Online (Step-by-Step)
metaDescription: Learn how to write off uncollectible customer debt in QuickBooks Online so your books stay accurate and you don't overstate income you'll never receive.
intro: When a customer never pays an invoice, leaving it open on your books overstates your income and muddies your financial picture. This guide covers how to write off bad debt in QuickBooks Online correctly, so your records reflect what you actually collected. It's aimed at business owners and bookkeepers who want clean, reconciled accounts without guesswork.

steps.heading: Confirm the debt is truly uncollectible
steps.body: Before writing anything off, make sure you've exhausted reasonable collection efforts on the outstanding invoice.

steps.heading: Create a bad debt expense account
steps.body: Set up an expense account specifically for bad debt if you don't already have one, so these write-offs are tracked separately from normal costs.

steps.heading: Apply a credit memo to the invoice
steps.body: Use a credit memo tied to the bad debt account to zero out the unpaid invoice without deleting the original transaction history.

steps.heading: Review the impact on your financials
steps.body: Check your profit and loss statement afterward to confirm the write-off is reflected and your income figures are now accurate.

steps.heading: Avoid common mistakes
steps.body: Don't simply delete the invoice — writing it off properly preserves your audit trail and keeps sales tax records consistent.

faq.question: Will writing off bad debt affect my sales tax liability?
faq.answer: It can, depending on how the original invoice was taxed, so it's worth reviewing with your accountant before finalizing the write-off.

faq.question: Can I undo a bad debt write-off if the customer eventually pays?
faq.answer: Yes, you can reverse the credit memo and record the payment normally if the customer later settles the balance.

faq.question: How often should businesses review outstanding invoices for bad debt?
faq.answer: Reviewing aging reports monthly or quarterly helps catch overdue invoices before they become long-term collection problems.

faq.question: Does this feature work the same for QuickBooks Online and Desktop?
faq.answer: The overall concept is similar, but the exact steps differ slightly between QuickBooks Online and Desktop versions.
